page.title: Mangusta 80 vs Ferretti 94: Which Luxury Yacht for Marbella?

data.meta_description: Mangusta 80 vs Ferretti 94 Marbella comparison: 24m vs 29m, 12 pax each, €4,719/4h vs price on request. Which luxury yacht suits your charter?
data.summary: A direct comparison of the Mangusta 80 and Ferretti 94 for Marbella charters, covering size, capacity, speed, inclusions, and pricing to help you choose the right yacht.
key_facts: Mangusta 80: 24m length, 12 pax, departs Puerto Banús, €4,719 for 4h (jet ski included).
Ferretti 94: 29m length, 12 pax, departs Puerto Banús, pricing on request via WhatsApp.
Both yachts include licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and 21% IVA.
Mangusta 80 has a sportier, open-deck design; Ferretti 94 offers more interior volume and flybridge.
Mangusta 80 is ideal for shorter, high-energy trips; Ferretti 94 suits longer, more relaxed cruises.

faq.q: Which is bigger, the Mangusta 80 or Ferretti 94?
faq.a: The Ferretti 94 is larger at 29m (95ft) compared to the Mangusta 80 at 24m (79ft). The Ferretti offers more interior space and a flybridge, while the Mangusta has a sportier, more open layout.

faq.q: How many guests can each yacht accommodate?
faq.a: Both the Mangusta 80 and Ferretti 94 can carry up to 12 guests. This makes them suitable for medium-sized groups, such as family outings or corporate events, without needing a larger vessel.

faq.q: What is the price difference between the Mangusta 80 and Ferretti 94?
faq.a: The Mangusta 80 has a fixed price of €4,719 for a minimum 4-hour charter, which includes a jet ski. The Ferretti 94 is priced on request, so you need to contact us via WhatsApp for a quote. Typically, larger yachts cost more per hour.

faq.q: Which yacht is better for a party or celebration?
faq.a: The Mangusta 80, with its open deck and included jet ski, is great for lively, action-packed trips. The Ferretti 94, with more interior space and a flybridge, offers a more refined party atmosphere, ideal for formal celebrations.

faq.q: Can I charter either yacht for a full day?
faq.a: Yes, both yachts can be chartered for longer durations. The Mangusta 80 has a minimum of 4 hours, but can be extended. For the Ferretti 94, you can discuss full-day options via WhatsApp. Longer charters allow you to visit places like Estepona or Sotogrande.

faq.q: What is included in the charter price for these yachts?
faq.a: Both charters include a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). The Mangusta 80 also includes a jet ski free of charge.

faq.q: Are there different versions of the Mangusta 80?
faq.a: Yes, we offer a Grey and a White version of the Mangusta 80. Both are 24m, carry 12 guests, and depart from Puerto Banús. The colour choice is aesthetic; performance and inclusions are identical.

The Mangusta 80, being lighter and more performance-oriented, generally achieves higher speeds than the Ferretti 94. However, exact speeds depend on sea conditions. For a thrilling ride, the Mangusta is preferred; for a leisurely cruise, the Ferretti excels.

data.body_html:
Choosing between the Mangusta 80 and the Ferretti 94 for your Marbella yacht charter is a matter of style, space, and budget. Both vessels depart from Puerto Banús and carry up to 12 guests, but they differ significantly in size, price, and onboard experience. In this Mangusta 80 vs Ferretti 94 Marbella comparison, we break down the key differences to help you decide which luxury yacht suits your day on the Costa del Sol.

Size and Layout: 24m vs 29m

The Mangusta 80 measures 24 metres (79 feet) and is designed for an open, sporty feel. Its layout prioritises outdoor socialising, with spacious sunbeds and a large aft deck. The Ferretti 94, at 29 metres (95 feet), offers a flybridge and significantly more interior volume, including a saloon, dining area, and multiple cabins. If you value interior comfort and a flybridge for panoramic views, the Ferretti wins. For a more intimate, deck-focused experience, choose the Mangusta.

Guest Capacity and Comfort

Both yachts accommodate a maximum of 12 passengers, making them ideal for groups of friends or family. The Ferretti 94 provides more lounging space, with separate zones for dining, sunbathing, and relaxing. The Mangusta 80, while slightly smaller, still offers generous deck space and a comfortable cabin. For a boat rental Marbella experience that emphasises sun and sea, the Mangusta is ample; for a more luxurious, multi-room feel, the Ferretti is superior.

Pricing and Inclusions

FeatureMangusta 80Ferretti 94
Length24m29m
Guests1212
Minimum Charter4 hoursOn request
Price€4,719 (includes jet ski)Price on request
InclusionsSkipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, IVASkipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, IVA

The Mangusta 80 has a transparent price of €4,719 for 4 hours, which includes a jet ski at no extra cost. The Ferretti 94 is priced on request — contact us via WhatsApp for a personalised quote. Both include a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, and 21% IVA. For more details on pricing, reach out directly.

Performance and Speed

The Mangusta 80 is built for speed and agility, with a sporty hull that delivers a thrilling ride. The Ferretti 94, while powerful, is designed for cruising comfort rather than outright speed. If you want to feel the wind in your hair and cover more coastline quickly, the Mangusta is your choice. For a smooth, relaxing journey with minimal motion, the Ferretti excels. Both can easily reach popular spots like Cabopino or Estepona.

Onboard Experience and Activities

The Mangusta 80 comes with a free jet ski, perfect for water sports enthusiasts. Its open deck makes it easy to jump into the sea for a swim. The Ferretti 94 offers a more refined experience, with a flybridge for sunbathing, a spacious saloon for dining, and a swim platform for easy water access. For a day packed with action, choose the Mangusta; for a luxurious, laid-back cruise, opt for the Ferretti. Both are available from Puerto Banús.

Which Yacht for Your Group?

Consider your group’s priorities. If you want a high-energy day with jet skiing and sunbathing, the Mangusta 80 is excellent value at a fixed price. If you seek a more spacious, elegant yacht with a flybridge for entertaining, the Ferretti 94 offers unmatched comfort. Both yachts are top-tier options for a Marbella boat charter, but the right choice depends on your budget and desired atmosphere.

Chartering from Puerto Banús

Both yachts depart from Puerto Banús, the heart of Marbella’s luxury scene. From here, you can cruise to the Golden Mile, Cala del Faro, or Cala Cortés. The Mangusta’s speed makes it ideal for reaching Sotogrande in under an hour, while the Ferretti is perfect for a slow journey along the coast. Whichever you choose, you’ll enjoy stunning views of La Concha mountain and the Mediterranean.

Frequently asked questions

Which is bigger, the Mangusta 80 or Ferretti 94?

The Ferretti 94 is larger at 29m (95ft) compared to the Mangusta 80 at 24m (79ft). The Ferretti offers more interior space and a flybridge, while the Mangusta has a sportier, more open layout.

How many guests can each yacht accommodate?

Both the Mangusta 80 and Ferretti 94 can carry up to 12 guests. This makes them suitable for medium-sized groups, such as family outings or corporate events, without needing a larger vessel.

What is the price difference between the Mangusta 80 and Ferretti 94?

The Mangusta 80 has a fixed price of €4,719 for a minimum 4-hour charter, which includes a jet ski. The Ferretti 94 is priced on request, so you need to contact us via WhatsApp for a quote. Typically, larger yachts cost more per hour.

Which yacht is better for a party or celebration?

The Mangusta 80, with its open deck and included jet ski, is great for lively, action-packed trips. The Ferretti 94, with more interior space and a flybridge, offers a more refined party atmosphere, ideal for formal celebrations.

Can I charter either yacht for a full day?

Yes, both yachts can be chartered for longer durations. The Mangusta 80 has a minimum of 4 hours, but can be extended. For the Ferretti 94, you can discuss full-day options via WhatsApp. Longer charters allow you to visit places like Estepona or Sotogrande.

What is included in the charter price for these yachts?

Both charters include a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). The Mangusta 80 also includes a jet ski free of charge.

Are there different versions of the Mangusta 80?

Yes, we offer a Grey and a White version of the Mangusta 80. Both are 24m, carry 12 guests, and depart from Puerto Banús. The colour choice is aesthetic; performance and inclusions are identical.

Which yacht is faster?

The Mangusta 80, being lighter and more performance-oriented, generally achieves higher speeds than the Ferretti 94. However, exact speeds depend on sea conditions. For a thrilling ride, the Mangusta is preferred; for a leisurely cruise, the Ferretti excels.
